meaning: vt.打扰,折磨,使狼狈,使担忧n.混乱,狼狈,忧虑
examples: Big concert halls do not faze Melanie.
He looked as if nothing could faze him.
The worst results cannot faze him.
This doesn't faze Roxanne Austin--who may not even have a job once DirecTV's fate is set.
That doesn't faze Garrard, who made a Pro Bowl in nine seasons with Jacksonville.
